step_description: Query Store plan changes during weekend maintenance window
rows: SELECT r.region, p.category, SUM(oi.qty*p.price) FROM OrderItems oi JOIN Products p ... GROUP BY r.region, p.category
Index rebuild on OrderItems updated statistics — row count estimate changed from 28M to 840M, causing 1000x memory grant inflation
SELECT o.order_id, o.total FROM Orders o WHERE o.status = @s AND o.created_date > @d
Statistics update on Orders — cardinality estimate changed (see plan regression scenario)
narrative_hint: The revenue report query (query_id 4820) changed its plan at 03:48 on Sunday during the index rebuild maintenance window. The OrderItems index rebuild updated statistics using a FULLSCAN which revealed the table had grown from 28M to 840M rows since statistics were last collected. The optimizer responded by inflating the memory grant from 28MB to 28,420MB — a 1000x increase — to accommodate the now-correctly-estimated row count. The plan itself is not wrong, but the memory grant is unsustainably large for concurrent execution. Claude should recommend: (1) add MAX_GRANT_PERCENT hint or Resource Governor classification to cap memory grants for the report query, (2) reduce parallelism with MAXDOP hint or workload group, (3) consider scheduling the report for off-peak hours.
